replies.author: @joshdoyle182
replies.text:
Unless the working capital in the world is divided into two parts, one of which is antiquated enough to be unimportant, and the other of which is only organized as banks, i.e. as antiquated as ancient Sumeria. That situation would make all existing corporations determined to create tediousness and ineptitude, rather than prosperity.

All I am saying is that is an empirical question. But it would explain both why Asians are consistently amazed that nobody else understands them, and Swiss claims about a "super-entity". And Climate change. And it would - hideously - make "corpse-poisons" a radically more ethical substitute.

author: @algirdaslazauskas2733
text:
A good point that commercial GO is expensive. However, it is worth buying one if you are trying to get a quality product of your own. Firstly, you would like to do that because the composition, structure, defects etc. can be easily checked using analytical techniques and compared with your own GO. Some tips: perform Energy-dispersive X-ray spectroscopy (EDS) on dry GO film (atomic % of carbon and oxygen is of the main interest) and Raman spectroscopy (D and G peaks around 1100-1700 cm-1 and 2D and D+G peaks around 2600-3000 cm-1).  Afterwards, you will get an idea of what you want to achieve. Further, you can identify (from EDS in some cases) what kind of stabilizing agent is used in GO dispersion (very important for high quality GO thin films).  Synthesis of GO is a tricky thing. I recommend to start from:
Ling Sun, Bunshi Fugetsu, Mass production of graphene oxide from expanded graphite, Materials Letters, Volume 109, 15 October 2013, Pages 207-210, ISSN 0167-577X, http://dx.doi.org/10.1016/j.matlet.2013.07.072. (http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0167577X13010100) Abstract: Abstract In a deviation from the conventional Hummers method, a spontaneous expansion approach was introduced with expanded graphite as the precursors. The intercalating agent (H2SO4) was able to penetrate into the expanded graphite; this had further expanded the graphite and as a result, a foam-like intermediate was produced. The foam-like graphite was more easily oxidized in reaction with the oxidant (KMnO4) to form graphene oxide (GO). Fully exfoliated GO was obtained with expanded graphite having the median diameter ∼15 μm as the precursors. This procedure was much safer and productive in scalable applications than the conventional Hummers methods. Keywords: Expanded graphite; Graphene oxide; Spontaneous expansion; Mass production

replies.author: @JonathanFosdickNano
replies.text: Yes, Hydrazine is basically rocket fuel. It is a VERY toxic, flammable, volatile liquid.  Even the vapors alone are dangerous and are lethal not to mention they are combustible and explosive. Going to ponder this for a few days before attempting to make any. I have dealt with some noxious and dangerous chemicals before, so proper safety must always come first and have all the PSE. If you do not know what PSE stands for, and had to google it, do NOT think about messing around with Hydrazine.  DO NOT attempt this at home, or in a lab unless you are a qualified and skilled chemist with only the proper equipment and safety procedures.

replies.author: @JonathanFosdickNano
replies.text: The only good thing about hydrazine is it is a very good solvent for graphene.

replies.text: Robert Murray-Smith Glad you liked it!  It's funny while I was making that same batch, I had burned out the thermofuse and a transistor in my blender as I was running it so long, and had to stop and glad to say I took it apart and got it sorted.  Yes, I agree, the average Joe should never mess about with such chemicals. :)  I bet having that lab license sure will make ordering chemicals a bit easier.
